name: Banded Standing Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Band tension rises as you come up onto the toes, making it hardest at peak contraction.
cues: Take up band slack before your first rep
Drive to full height and squeeze the top
Keep the knees straight for the gastrocnemius
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with the balls of both feet, feet hip-width apart.
Hold an end of the band in each hand and raise your hands to shoulder height to take up the slack.
Stand tall with knees straight and light tension already on the band.
Rise onto the balls of your feet to full plantarflexion against the band.
Squeeze hard at the top, then lower your heels under control.
good_when: You train at home or while travelling and want a joint-friendly calf stimulus
You want extra resistance at peak contraction to complement stretch-biased machine work
bad_when: You need heavy overload - bands cap out quickly for strong calves
The band gives too little tension at the stretched bottom to challenge you there
common_errors: Letting the band go slack and losing tension
Short reps that never reach full plantarflexion
progressions: Use a thicker band or double it up
Progress to a single-leg banded raise
